content: (The location is now The Winking Skeever.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Lydia, Gulum-Ei, Frea, Belrand, Lisette)

Corpulus Vinius: Welcome to the Winking Skeever, even at this unholy hour. You lot look like you've trudged through half of Skyrim—pull up a stool, I'll pour something to warm those bones. Serana: Why thank you, barkeep—nothing says 'welcome' like stumbling into a tavern at 3 AM, unless you're the ghost of hangovers past. Make mine something with a kick; we've got stories that'd make your mead curdle.

Corpulus Vinius: Stories to curdle mead, eh? Sounds like you've dodged more than a few dragons out there. Coming right up—honning mead with a dash of honey, unless you fancy something stronger from the back shelf. What's brought this motley crew to Solitude's doorstep so late? Serana: Motley crew? Flattering, barkeep—last time I heard that, it was from a draugr who thought my fangs were part of the band. We've been chasing shadows colder than this tavern's midnight glow; pour generously, and maybe I'll spill how the Empire's grip feels looser than a Thalmor's conscience these days.

Corpulus Vinius: Empire's grip, you say? Aye, the civil war's got everyone whispering, but in here, it's just mead and merriment keeping the chill out. That fire's crackling nicely tonight—warm enough to chase away the Solitude fog rolling in at this hour. What's your take on the Thalmor meddling? Serana: Thalmor? Oh, they're like that ex who shows up uninvited, preaching purity while eyeing the mead stash. Me, I'd rather share the warmth by the hearth—imagine thawing those frostbitten bones with a little skooma-laced story or two, especially with the way the shadows dance like they're hiding secrets in this cozy den.

Corpulus Vinius: Secrets in the shadows, eh? This old place has its share—those arches have heard more whispers than the Bards College on a busy night. At 3 AM, with the fog pressing in outside, it's just us and the fire's glow to keep the ghosts at bay. Care for a refill while you spin your tales by the hearth? Serana: Ghosts? Honey, I've dated worse—eternal boredom's the real killer. But this vaulted den at midnight's edge feels like a cozy crypt party, with the flames licking the stone like they're thirsty for more than just logs. Pass the cups; let's toast to ditching the Thalmor's sermons for something steamier, like sharing the warmth under these ancient arches.
Corpulus Vinius: Aye, toast away by that hearth—its flames are dancing like they know the hour's secrets at 3 AM, with the fog outside turning Solitude into a ghost town.

Corpulus Vinius: A B. F. , eh? Coming right up—Black-Briar Fine, I reckon, straight from Riften's finest. That'll chase the 3 AM Solitude chill right out of ya. Serana: B. F.? Careful, love, in my line of work that sounds like a bad blind date with a frostbite finish—pour mine strong too, barkeep; nothing wakes the night like a sip that bites back harder than my fangs under these flickering tavern lights.

Corpulus Vinius: There you are—Black-Briar Fine, smooth as Solitude's midnight fog rolling off the bay. Settles the nerves after a long trek, especially with that hearth roaring away like it's got stories of its own in these stone arches. Serana: Smooth indeed, barkeep—slides down like a lover's whisper in the dead of night, fog or no fog outside. Let's let the fire crackle on while we nurse these; no rush to unravel the Empire's knots when the mead's this fine under the tavern's cozy glow.

(*You are observing the following:* In this dimly lit stone chamber with vaulted arches and rough-hewn walls, warm flickering light emanates from a modest hearth where a fire crackles, casting long shadows across the uneven flagstone floor. A wooden table stands near the fire, laden with a few scattered items like a small pot and candle holders, one of which holds a gently burning taper. Seated at the table is a figure with long, pale hair cascading down their back, dressed in simple, layered garments that suggest travel-worn attire; they appear absorbed in examining an open book or parchment spread before them, one hand resting on the surface. Nearby, a couple of sturdy wooden chairs frame the table, and a woven rug softens part of the floor beneath. In the foreground, your own hands are visible, gloved and extended slightly, as if reaching toward the scene or holding a small object. Scattered around the edges are alcoves with hanging herbs or cloth bundles, adding to the intimate, lived-in feel of the space.)
(The location is now The Winking Skeever.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Gulum-Ei, Lisette, Minette Vinius) (Serana stood up from a(n) Wooden Bar Stool)
Corpulus Vinius: Good morning, folks! Dawn's broken through the fog outside—looks like a crisp one in Solitude today. More mead to start the day right? Minette's just wiping down the counters.

Lydia: My Thane! What sorcery is this? Dibella's light, you'll burn the whole Skeever down—put it out before these Imperial fools call the guards! Serana: Oh, darling, dual flames at breakfast? That's one way to wake up the dead... or just Corpulus's profits. Careful, or you'll singe those pretty gloves. Frea: All-Maker preserve us, that fire and spirit energy—it's like Solstheim's dual storms in one grasp! Channel it wisely, Dragonborn, lest it draws Miraak's echoes even here in this stone hall. Belrand: By the gods, that's some spellwork! If you're hiring for that kind of firepower, count me in—better than rotting in this tavern all day. Corpulus Vinius: Whoa there, friend! No magic shows in the Winking Skeever before I've swept the floors—put that out quick, or it's on your tab for any scorch marks!
Lydia: Thane, by Dibella's curves, enough with the flames!

(*You are observing the following:* In this dimly lit stone chamber, rough-hewn walls curve into arched alcoves, their surfaces textured with uneven masonry and faint mossy patches. To the right, a recessed niche holds a framed portrait of a woman with pale skin and dark hair, her expression serene yet enigmatic, illuminated by the soft glow of nearby candles. A cluster of delicate pink cherry blossoms spills from a simple clay pot on the floor beside it, their petals contrasting against the cool gray stone. In the foreground, a small wooden stool sits on the uneven flagstone floor, beside a low table bearing a white ceramic lamp where a single candle flickers, casting warm shadows and droplets of wax. Nearby, a potted plant with slender purple irises adds a touch of subtle color to the austere surroundings. The room's center draws the eye to a small, ethereal fire—crackling red and orange flames that lick upward from the cracks in the stone, as if conjured from embers, with faint sparks dancing briefly before fading. A woven blue rug partially covers the floor near the flames, its edges frayed and blending into the shadows. The overall atmosphere is one of quiet antiquity, with light sources creating pools of warmth amid the encroaching darkness.)
